description: 'melu' is a friend, someone joined to another person by a chosen relation of affection or trust. Friendship is mutual when both people hold it; one speaker calling someone a friend does not announce the other person's view. 'keloe' (companion) requires only shared company or a course travelled together.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', let the sound resonate through the nose, and release into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Both consonants keep the voice running, first through the nose and then along the sides of the tongue. The short form changes contact without a hard break. It is an easy sound to call across a room.
usage_notes: Possessor-first 'mia melu' identifies my friend without claiming ownership. Reciprocal 'wiso' can make a mutually directed act explicit, while the surrounding conversation can establish whether both people name the relation alike.

semantic_domains.community: a chosen interpersonal relation recognised as friendship
semantic_domains.emotion: friendship commonly carries affection or personal trust
